description:
You are working as a script-developer who has just checked out a small practice
  repository in  

    /home/user/dev-utils  

  Inside that directory you will already find two data files that simulate the
  daily feed coming from other systems:  

    • /home/user/dev-utils/raw_ips.log  
    • /home/user/dev-utils/users.csv  

  1) Using **sed**, extract every IPv4 address that occurs in  
     /home/user/dev-utils/raw_ips.log, keep only the address itself (remove
     everything else), remove duplicates, and sort the remaining addresses in
     strictly ascending numerical order (i.e. “8.8.8.8” comes before
     “10.0.0.5”).  
     • Write the final list **one IP per line, no extra
       whitespace** to  

         /home/user/dev-utils/sanitized_ips.txt

  2) Using **awk**, analyse /home/user/dev-utils/users.csv and create a summary
     of how many *active* users belong to each department.  
     • Only users whose status field is the literal word “active”
       (case-sensitive) should be counted.  
     • Produce a tab-separated output file (department, then count) sorted
       ① by the count in **descending** order and ② for equal counts by the
       department name in **ascending** alphabetical order.  
     • Save the result to  

         /home/user/dev-utils/dept_summary.tsv

  3) Compose a human-readable daily report that combines both results exactly in
     the following format (pay close attention to the literal separators and
     headings — the checker is strict):  

         === Sanitized IP Addresses ===
         <the IP list as it appears in sanitized_ips.txt>
         === Department Active Users ===
         <the department summary exactly as in dept_summary.tsv>

     There must be *no* blank lines before, after, or between the two
     sections.  Save this file to  

         /home/user/dev-utils/daily_report_2024-02-10.txt

  4) For traceability, append two lines to a processing log file
     /home/user/dev-utils/process.log (create it if it does not yet exist).
     They must read *exactly*:

         [OK] Sanitized 12 unique IPv4 addresses from raw_ips.log
         [OK] Produced department summary for 4 departments

     No timestamp, no extra spaces, one linefeed at the end of the second line.

  When everything is done, the directory /home/user/dev-utils must therefore
  contain, in addition to the original two data files, exactly the following
  new files (all plain text, UTF-8 encoded):

     sanitized_ips.txt
     dept_summary.tsv
     daily_report_2024-02-10.txt
     process.log

  The automated test suite will open and verify the **contents** of each file,
  as well as their presence.  Make sure every step is performed with standard
  Unix tools (awk, sed, sort, uniq, etc.) available in a typical Linux shell.
truth:
The container is pre-populated with the following two files and their exact
  contents:

  1) /home/user/dev-utils/raw_ips.log
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  2024-02-09 12:01:11 Connection from 192.168.1.10: accepted
  2024-02-09 12:01:12 8.8.8.8 requested /favicon.ico
  WARN: suspicious activity from 10.0.0.5
  Client at 172.16.0.3 disconnected
  192.168.1.12 - - [09/Feb/2024:12:01:13] "GET /index.html"
  203.0.113.25 - possible abuse report
  INFO [client 10.1.2.3] request processed
  192.168.1.10 - - [09/Feb/2024:12:01:15] "GET /dashboard"
  ERROR - invalid credentials from 10.0.0.5
  Ping from 192.168.1.100 successful
  Session start for 100.64.0.1
  198.51.100.42 contacted support endpoint
  Heartbeat ok from 172.20.14.2
  Random noise without address
  Client 192.0.2.123 disconnected
  Another line with IP 192.168.1.12
  ----------------------------------------------------------------

  2) /home/user/dev-utils/users.csv
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  id,name,department,status
  1,Alice Smith,Engineering,active
  2,Bob Jones,Engineering,active
  3,Carol White,Engineering,inactive
  4,David Black,HR,active
  5,Eva Green,Marketing,active
  6,Frank Brown,Sales,active
  7,Grace Miller,Sales,active
  8,Hank Wilson,Sales,active
  9,Ian Clark,Marketing,inactive
  10,Judy Adams,Engineering,active
  11,Kate Lewis,Engineering,active
  12,Larry Young,Engineering,active
  13,Mona Hall,HR,inactive
  14,Nick King,Marketing,active
  ----------------------------------------------------------------

  From these inputs the correct solutions are:

  • /home/user/dev-utils/sanitized_ips.txt
    (12 lines, Unix line endings, strictly ascending)
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  8.8.8.8
  10.0.0.5
  10.1.2.3
  100.64.0.1
  172.16.0.3
  172.20.14.2
  192.0.2.123
  192.168.1.10
  192.168.1.12
  192.168.1.100
  198.51.100.42
  203.0.113.25
  ----------------------------------------------------------------

  • /home/user/dev-utils/dept_summary.tsv
    (tab-separated, no header)
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  Engineering	5
  Sales	3
  Marketing	2
  HR	1
  ----------------------------------------------------------------

  • /home/user/dev-utils/daily_report_2024-02-10.txt
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  === Sanitized IP Addresses ===
  8.8.8.8
  10.0.0.5
  10.1.2.3
  100.64.0.1
  172.16.0.3
  172.20.14.2
  192.0.2.123
  192.168.1.10
  192.168.1.12
  192.168.1.100
  198.51.100.42
  203.0.113.25
  === Department Active Users ===
  Engineering	5
  Sales	3
  Marketing	2
  HR	1
  ----------------------------------------------------------------

  • /home/user/dev-utils/process.log
    (exactly two lines, each ending in a single LF)
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  [OK] Sanitized 12 unique IPv4 addresses from raw_ips.log
  [OK] Produced department summary for 4 departments
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
